chunks.question: What are the titles and authors of the first ten technical reports from 1966?
chunks.answer: Shadows of Fuzzy Sets by Zadeh to Constrained Minimization by Da Cunha and Polak.

chunks.text: Identification of Linear Discrete Time Systems Using the Instrumental Variable Method (M187) by K.Y. Wong and E. Polak. Controllability and Observability (M186) by C.T. Chen and C.A. Desoer. Integrated Lowpass Amplifiers (M185) by Arthur J. Brodersen. Self-Duality and Constant Resistance One-Ports (M184) by C.A. Desoer and K.K. Wong. Bounded-Input Bounded-Output Stability of Nonlinear Time-Varying Discrete Control Systems (M183) by J.C. Lin and P.P. Varaiya. Invertibility, Equivalence, and Decomposition Property of Abstract Systems (M181) by K. Doty and H. Frank. An Extremal Problem in Banach Space with Application to Optimal Control (M180) by P.P. Varaiya. Microwave X-band Faraday Rotation of Hemoglobin with Adsorbed Water (M179) by Soo-Young Chai and Paul O. Vogelhut. On the Trajectories of a Differential System (M178) by P.P. Varaiya.
chunks.question: What topics are covered in technical reports M187 to M178?
chunks.answer: System identification, control theory, amplifiers, stability, and differential systems.

chunks.question: What are the key areas in reports M177 to M168?
chunks.answer: Differential systems, image representation, synchronization, antennas, and amplifiers.
